38: /**
39:  * Utils to create logback appenders
40:  */
41: public class AppenderUtils {
42: 
43:   public static final String PATH_SEP = "/";
44: 
45:   private AppenderUtils() {
46:   }
47: 
48:   /**
49:    * Encoder for LogMessage objects.
50:    */
51:   public static class LogMessageEncoder extends EncoderBase<LogMessage> {
52: 
53:     private TTransport framedTransport;
54:     private TProtocol protocol;
55:     private OutputStream os;
56: 
57:     @Override
58:     public void init(OutputStream os) {
59:       this.os = os;
60:       // Use the TFlushingFastFramedTransport to be compatible with singer_thrift
61:       // log.
62:       final int bufferCapacity = 10;
63:       framedTransport = new TFastFramedTransport(new TIOStreamTransport(os),
64:           bufferCapacity);
65:       protocol = new TBinaryProtocol(framedTransport);
66:     }
67: 
68:     @Override
69:     public void doEncode(LogMessage logMessage) throws IOException {
70:       try {
71:         logMessage.write(protocol);
72:         framedTransport.flush();
73:       } catch (TException e) {
74:         throw new IOException(e);
75:       }
76:     }
77: 
78:     @Override
79:     public void close() throws IOException {
80:       framedTransport.close();
81:     }
82:   }
83: 
84:   /**
85:    * Create the basic thrift appender which logs to a file
86:    * and rolls the file when it exceeds a certain size.
87:    *
88:    * @param basePath base directory the files are under.
89:    * @param topic the topic name for the current appender.
90:    * @param rotateThresholdKBytes threshold in kilobytes to rotate after.
91:    * @param context the logback context.
92:    */
93:   public static Appender<LogMessage> createFileRollingThriftAppender(
94:       File basePath,
95:       String topic,
96:       long rotateThresholdKBytes,
97:       Context context,
98:       int maxRetentionHours) {
99:     RollingFileAppender<LogMessage> appender = new RollingFileAppender<LogMessage>();
100:     appender.setContext(context);
101:     appender.setAppend(true);
102:     appender.setPrudent(false);
103: 
104:     LogMessageEncoder encoder = new LogMessageEncoder();
105:     appender.setEncoder(encoder);
106:     appender.setFile(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic);
107: 
108:     TimeBasedRollingPolicy policy = new TimeBasedRollingPolicy();
109:     policy.setMaxHistory(maxRetentionHours);
110:     policy.setFileNamePattern(basePath + PATH_SEP + topic + ".%d{yyyy-MM-dd-HH}.%i");
111:     policy.setCleanHistoryOnStart(false);
112:     policy.setContext(context);
113:     policy.setParent(appender);
114: 
115:     // Also impose a max size per file policy.
116:     SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP fnatp = new SizeAndTimeBasedFNATP();
117:     fnatp.setContext(context);
118:     fnatp.setTimeBasedRollingPolicy(policy);
119:     fnatp.setMaxFileSize(FileSize.valueOf(String.format("%sKB", rotateThresholdKBytes)));
120: 
121:     policy.setTimeBasedFileNamingAndTriggeringPolicy(fnatp);
122:     appender.setRollingPolicy(policy);
123:     appender.setTriggeringPolicy(policy);
124: 
125:     policy.start();
126:     appender.start();
127: 
128:     return appender;
129:   }
130: }

48:

50:                 .filter(path -> path.toString().endsWith(".java"))

60:     private static void rewriteFile(JavaParser parser, Path file) throws IOException {
61:         Optional<CompilationUnit> parsed = parser.parse(file).getResult();
62:         if (parsed.isEmpty()) {
63:             return;
64:         }
65: 
66:         CompilationUnit cu = parsed.get();
67:         LexicalPreservingPrinter.setup(cu);
68: 
69:         cu.findAll(MethodDeclaration.class).forEach(Main::rewriteCallable);
70:         cu.findAll(ConstructorDeclaration.class).forEach(Main::rewriteCallable);
71: 
72:         rewriteImports(cu);
73:         rewriteGlobalTypeNames(cu);
74: 
75:         String rewritten = LexicalPreservingPrinter.print(cu);
76:         Files.writeString(file, rewritten, StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
77:     }

80:         cu.getImports().forEach(importDecl -> {
81:             String name = importDecl.getNameAsString();

95:         cu.findAll(ObjectCreationExpr.class).forEach(creation -> {
96:             if (matchesType(creation.getType(), OLD_FNATP) || matchesType(creation.getType(), OLD_FNATP_FQN)) {
97:                 creation.setType(NEW_POLICY);
98:             }
99:         });
100:     }
101: 
102:     private static void rewriteCallable(MethodDeclaration method) {
103:         method.getBody().ifPresent(body -> rewriteBlock(body));
104:     }
105: 
106:     private static void rewriteCallable(ConstructorDeclaration constructor) {
107:         constructor.getBody().ifPresent(body -> rewriteBlock(body));
108:     }

110:     private static void rewriteBlock(com.github.javaparser.ast.stmt.BlockStmt body) {
111:         Map<String, String> fnatpToPolicy = new HashMap<>();
112:         Set<String> policyVars = new HashSet<>();
113:         Set<String> fnatpVars = new HashSet<>();
114:         List<ExpressionStmt> statementsToRemove = new ArrayList<>();
115: 
116:         body.findAll(VariableDeclarator.class).forEach(vd -> {
117:             if (matchesType(vd.getType(), OLD_ROLLING_POLICY) || matchesType(vd.getType(), OLD_ROLLING_POLICY_FQN)) {
118:                 policyVars.add(vd.getNameAsString());
119:             }
120:             if (matchesType(vd.getType(), OLD_FNATP) || matchesType(vd.getType(), OLD_FNATP_FQN)) {
121:                 fnatpVars.add(vd.getNameAsString());
122:             }
123:         });
124: 
125:         body.findAll(MethodCallExpr.class).forEach(call -> {
126:             if (call.getNameAsString().equals(SET_FILE_NAMING_AND_TRIGGERING_POLICY)) {
127:                 extractName(call.getScope()).ifPresent(scopeName -> {
128:                     if (!call.getArguments().isEmpty()) {
129:                         extractName(call.getArgument(0)).ifPresent(argName -> {
130:                             fnatpToPolicy.put(argName, scopeName);
131:                             policyVars.add(scopeName);
132:                             fnatpVars.add(argName);
133:                         });
134:                     }
135:                 });
136:             }
137:         });
138: 
139:         body.findAll(VariableDeclarator.class).forEach(vd -> {
140:             if (policyVars.contains(vd.getNameAsString()) &&
141:                     (matchesType(vd.getType(), OLD_ROLLING_POLICY) || matchesType(vd.getType(), OLD_ROLLING_POLICY_FQN))) {
142:                 vd.setType(NEW_POLICY);

152:                     if (matchesType(creation.getType(), OLD_ROLLING_POLICY) || matchesType(creation.getType(), OLD_ROLLING_POLICY_FQN)
153:                             || matchesType(creation.getType(), OLD_FNATP) || matchesType(creation.getType(), OLD_FNATP_FQN)) {

161:             String methodName = call.getNameAsString();
162:             Optional<String> scopeName = extractName(call.getScope());

179:                 }

181:         });

183:         statementsToRemove.forEach(ExpressionStmt::remove);

194:         if (expression.isNameExpr()) {

**Exploring encoder methods**

I’m thinking we need to continue with the project and consider using apply_patch to add encoder methods. It seems like the compilation errors might actually be in the Singer project, which could mean there's a bunch to fix there. We might be able to fix AppenderUtils directly, but the user is asking for a more generic reusable transformation. 

I wonder if we can rewrite EncoderBase subclasses by adding headerBytes and footerBytes, and renaming doEncode to encode. Implementing a generic AST manipulation with JavaParser could be complex, so I need to inspect the API for MethodDeclaration and see how to add methods. Maybe we can use addMember to do that. I’m also curious if we should account for constructors or create parsed snippets. It might be easier to use StaticJavaParser.parseBodyDeclaration, but I definitely want to check the documentation to confirm whether to use StaticJavaParser.parseBodyDeclaration or StaticJavaParser.parseMethodDeclaration. Let's look into that.
I’m extending the rule to cover the encoder API change too, since that’s the remaining project failure after the rolling-policy rewrite.
